discovery_context: Running scripts/verify-all-constitution-rules.sh in background during §6.AD-debt closure cycle (commits 11820734 + 977630c3 + 7dc20171). Sweep hung for 5+ minutes inside hermetic-check-constitution-check_constitution_test gate at 0% CPU per ps. Investigation found a running `cp -r /Users/milosvasic/Projects/Lava/. /var/folders/.../tmp.XXX/` child process — copying the entire monorepo (including 16 submodules + all build outputs + .git histories + .claude/worktrees) for each test fixture.
discoverer: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) — Lava 1.2.30-1050 tooling cycle, agent-driven
constitutional_classification: performance discovery, not a behavior bluff — the tests produce CORRECT results; they just take 5+ minutes each. Multiple per-test cp_-r calls cumulatively make the gate impractical to run routinely.
constitutional_clauses_implicated: §6.T.2 (Resource Limits for Tests & Challenges) — the cp -r pattern uses unbounded disk + I/O for repo-size baseline)

evidence.fixture_calls: make_fixture() invoked once per test (test_missing_6n_heading_fails, test_missing_6n_from_submodule_fails, test_missing_check4_marker_fails, etc. — each does a full repo cp)
evidence.main_disk_pressure_amplifier: /var/folders/.../tmp/ is on macOS main disk; main disk at this moment had only 5G-65G headroom because of separate USB-T7 migration in progress; cp into the same disk amplifies the disk-pressure scenario

remediation_options.label: Option A — Exclude heavy dirs
remediation_options.description: Change line 43 to `rsync -a --exclude=.git --exclude=build --exclude=.claude --exclude=releases --exclude=node_modules --exclude='submodules/*/build' "$REPO_ROOT/" "$fixture/"`. Drops cp time from ~5min to ~30s based on relative sizes (build outputs + .git + .claude/worktrees are the bulk).
remediation_options.risk: Low. The tests are grep-based on CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md + CONSTITUTION.md content — none of the excluded dirs affect parse outcomes.

remediation_options.label: Option B — Add LAVA_REPO_ROOT support to scripts/check-constitution.sh
remediation_options.description: Refactor check-constitution.sh to honor LAVA_REPO_ROOT env (current implementation hardcodes REPO_ROOT from script dir). Then the test can point at a SMALL synthetic fixture without copying the real repo. Same pattern as scripts/check-script-docs-sync.sh + scripts/check-commit-docs-exists.sh.
remediation_options.risk: Medium. check-constitution.sh is 700+ lines with many path resolutions; need to ensure every internal path becomes relative to REPO_ROOT consistently.

remediation_options.label: Option C — Cached fixture per test session
remediation_options.description: Build the fixture ONCE at suite start, snapshot to a baseline, then for each test do a cheap rsync-based revert from baseline. Reduces N cp calls to 1.
remediation_options.risk: Medium. Requires test isolation discipline — if a test pollutes the baseline, subsequent tests would inherit the pollution.

recommended_remediation: Option A first (15-min win that returns sweep wall-time to acceptable). Then Option B as a deeper refactor when the next phase touches check-constitution.sh. Option C only if Option B proves intractable.
operator_actions_required: Confirm preferred remediation (A vs B vs hybrid).
Per §6.M Class III: this is performance not stability; no host-stability response needed.
session_outcome: Discovery recorded as forensic anchor. Sweep was killed mid-run to unblock the commit pipeline (PID 61549 terminated; cp PID 22679 explicitly killed). Fresh sweep launched at PID 23416 (will hit the same issue when it reaches the same test). Both new gates (CM-SCRIPT-DOCS-SYNC + CM-COMMIT-DOCS-EXISTS) PASS in the fresh sweep before the slow gate.
tracking_task_id: lava-1.2.31-cycle (or later) — apply Option A remediation
